mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, defined as the unemployment rate increasing by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months, signals an impending recession that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >70% of instances following a 0.5pp unemployment spike, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear markets with a t-stat > 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction driven by price shocks, which compresses corporate margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and falling RSAFS yields positive 20-day equity returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accommodative monetary policy, a month-over-month expansion in industrial production reflects strengthening economic activity that boosts near-term equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The positive relationship between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward returns reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or the signal produces negative alpha in >55% of easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as market participants price in higher fundamental cash flows.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized, or the rolling correlation between GDPC1 growth and equity returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation surges above 0.8%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.8% threshold, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment rises while the unemployment rate falls confirms a robust labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, which precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 60-day equity returns in >65% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when GDPC1 growth remains above 3%.
